content: Standard pixel-to-real-world calibration: photograph a ruler/object of known length in the same imaging session/setup, measure that known distance in pixels (line selection), and compute scale factor as known_distance / pixel_distance (e.g. ImageJ Analyze>Set Scale). This produces a pixels-per-unit (or unit-per-pixel) conversion factor scoped to one imaging session — matches the project's requirement to compute one conversion factor per shoot/session and join it to that session's thread measurements by date.
